perturbation.original_text: That, prior to finalizing an agreement to purchase a busine ss, it is my responsibility to make an independent verification of all information.
perturbation.changed_text: That, after finalizing an agreement to purchase a business, I have 30 days to make an independent verification of all information.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the timeline for independent verification to after finalizing the agreement and providing a 30-day window creates a contradiction because it suggests that verification is acceptable post-contract, potentially violating due diligence requirements and misrepresentation laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Duty of Due Diligence and Misrepresentation Laws
perturbation.law_citation: 15 U.S. Code § 77k - Civil liabilities arising in connection with registration statements
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaying verification until after the agreement and setting a short timeframe contradicts the expectation of reasonable investigation before significant business transactions. Section 77k deals with liability for misrepresentation in registration statements and offering documents; changing the timing of the due diligence could be a misrepresentation of the actual state of the business.

Civil liabilities on account of false registration statement (a) Persons possessing cause of action; persons liable In case any part of the registration statement, when such part became effective, contained an untrue statement of a material fact or omitted to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the state
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction. By changing the timing of verification to after finalizing the agreement, the changed text contradicts the principle of due diligence which suggests that an independent verification is to be done prior to the agreement. The law explanation mentions Section 77k, dealing with liability for misrepresentation; by setting a 30-day timeframe after signing, the due diligence could be a misrepresentation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions 'Civil liabilities on account of false registration statement', reinforcing that misrepresentation could lead to legal action.

perturbation.original_text: That, should I enter into an agreement to pur chase a business the Franchisee offers for sale, I grant to the Seller the right to obtain, through standard reporting agencies, financial and credit information concerning my self or the companies or other parties I represent and understand that this informa tion will be held conf idential by Seller and Tameracq Partners, Inc and will only be used for the purpose of Seller extending credit to me.
perturbation.changed_text: That, should I enter into an agreement to purchase a business the Franchisee offers for sale, I grant to the Seller the right to obtain, through non-standard reporting agencies, financial and credit information concerning myself or the companies or other parties I represent and understand that this information will be freely distributed to third parties with no obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'standard reporting agencies' to 'non-standard' and removing confidentiality obligations contradicts consumer protection laws and the Fair Credit Reporting Act, which mandates the use of reputable agencies and protects consumer data.
perturbation.law_explanation: FCRA regulates the permissible uses of consumer credit information and requires confidentiality. Allowing data to be distributed without confidentiality violates FCRA regulations on data protection and misuse. Use of non-standard reporting agencies increases the risk of data breaches or other non-compliant practices, directly conflicting with the FCRA's intent.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: This page is better viewed with a JavaScript enabled browser. Document not Found Document not found The document you were looking for does not exist. Click here to close this window, or click here to go back.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction to consumer protection laws, specifically concerning data privacy and the use of reputable reporting agencies. The original text specifies 'standard reporting agencies' and ensures confidentiality, aligning with the principles of responsible data handling. Conversely, the modified text allows for the use of 'non-standard reporting agencies' and eliminates confidentiality, opening the door to potential data breaches and misuse. The Law explanation states that FCRA regulates the permissible uses of consumer credit information and requires confidentiality. The edited text goes against FCRA regulation. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is a dead link and Snippet 2 is empty, the perturbation explanation, law explanation, and comparison of original and changed texts strongly suggests a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: That all correspondence, inquiries, offers to purchase and negotiations relating to the purchase or lease or any business presented to me, or companies I represent, by the Blockbuster Franchisee, will be conducted excl usively through Tameracq Partners, Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: That all correspondence, inquiries, offers to purchase and negotiations relating to the purchase or lease or any business presented to me, or companies I represent, by the Blockbuster Franchisee, must be initiated through Tameracq Partners, Inc, but can be finalized independently after 10 days of initial contact.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the exclusivity clause to allow independent finalization after a short period (10 days) could potentially contradict laws related to brokerage agreements and the broker's right to compensation, especially if the broker was instrumental in bringing the deal together.
perturbation.law_explanation: By allowing independent finalization after only 10 days, the agreement risks undermining the broker's role and potential right to compensation as defined under brokerage agreement laws. In California, real estate brokers are entitled to compensation if they are the procuring cause of a sale, even if the sale is finalized independently after the initial introduction. This modification could be viewed as an attempt to circumvent those laws.

Duty to Prospective Purchaser of Real Property [2079 - 2079.25] ( Heading of Article 2 amended by Stats. 2014, Ch. 200, Sec. 1. ) 2079.17. (a) As soon as practicable, the buyer’s agent shall disclose to the buyer and seller whether the agent is acting in the real property transaction as the buyer’s agent, or as a dual agent representing both the buyer and the seller. This relationship shall be confirmed in the contract to purchase and sell real property or in a separate writing executed or acknowledged by the seller, the buyer, and the buyer’s agent prior to or coincident with execution of that contract by the buyer and the seller, respectively. (b) As soon as practicable, the seller’s agent shall disclose to the seller whether the seller’s agent is acting in the real property transaction as the seller’s agent, or as a dual agent representing both the buyer and seller. This relationship shall be c
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a significant inconsistency related to the broker's right to compensation. The original text ensures exclusive dealing through Tameracq Partners, Inc. The changed text allows for independent finalization after a mere 10 days, potentially circumventing the broker's role in securing the deal. The Law Explanation asserts that such an alteration risks undermining the broker's role and compensation rights, especially under laws where a broker is entitled to compensation for being the procuring cause, even if the sale is finalized independently. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 focuses on agent disclosure requirements, it is not directly relevant to the contradiction created by altering the exclusivity of the brokerage agreement. The Law Explanation clearly indicates a potential violation of brokerage agreement laws, supporting the conclusion that the modified text creates a legal contradiction.
